message:
So there I was at 11 p.m., phone at 2%, trying to film my kids practicing our family fire escape plan. Of course the battery died mid-video. 🙃

But you know what? We'd already walked through it twice that week, so they knew the route, the meeting spot, everything. The phone thing didn't matter because the *actual* plan was already in their heads.

That's when it hit me: all the prep in the world doesn't help if it lives only on a screen. Whether it's a dead phone, a power outage, or just a moment of panic—your family needs to *know* the plan, not just have it saved somewhere.

Working smoke alarms, a practiced exit route, a spot to meet outside... none of that requires WiFi or batteries.

Have you and your family talked through your escape plan out loud? What's your meeting spot?
